title: Sporobolus airoides
taxonomy: Sporobolus airoides is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Sporoboleae and is a type of cool-season grass. This species is native to North America and is commonly known as sand dropseed. Sporobolus airoides is further classified as a perennial grass that grows in a variety of environments, including sandy soils and dry prairies. It is a relatively small grass, typically growing to a height of one to two feet.
uses: Sporobolus airoides, also known as Airoid Dropseed, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin infections.
